title: Fish Scenes, Symbolism, and Kingship in the Bas-Reliefs of Angkor ...

title: KHMER BAS-RELIEFS AT ANGKOR WAT AND BAYON
highlights: crossing the Tonle Sap. The scene is immortalized in stone, of course, because the Khmer  ...    The bas-reliefs at Bayon are within two galleries. The inner one is decorated with mythical scenes. The bas-reliefs on the outer gallery are a marked departure from anything previously seen at Angkor. They contain genre scenes of everyday  ...    identified by umbrellas, mounted on elephants. The quality of the bas-reliefs is good but not as fine as that at Angkor Wat however they show more humor and wit and give more insight into the everyday life of the ancient Khmers. In the South Gallery are images of everyday life that depict fortunetellers, hospitals, taverns, markets, fishing, festivals, jugglers, beauty parlors, women giving birth, men fishing in Tonle Sap, bookies taking bets at a cockfight, monks trying to remove the sarong of a young girl, and a man pulling out his whiskers with tweezers. There are also  ...    epic the Ramayana. EAST: A) Left, Water festival; two ships
